text: The Tao that can be trodden is not the enduring and unchanging Tao. The name that can be named is not the enduring and unchanging name.
author: Laozi
source: Project Gutenberg eBook #216 (Legge's translation; the same text as Sacred Books of the East v39, 1891)
out_pulls_note: High heat: the apophatic paradox makes a stranger stop -- it tells you in two lines that the deepest thing slips the instant you name it.
search_notes: James Legge translation, 1891 (his SBE v39 rendering; Gutenberg #216 is titled 'The Tao Teh King' and credits Translator: James Legge). sacred-texts.com (the SBE scan) is Cloudflare-blocked to curl, so I used the byte-clean Gutenberg plain text of Legge's same translation. Verbatim substring confirmed after normalising whitespace; no italic underscores in the span. Canonical ch.1 opening, the standard jolt. Confidence high.
